findings.description: The instruction page infographic is completely missing from the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a detailed infographic with 'Instructions for Handwritten Forms' containing Guidelines, Printing, and Before Sending sections with icons and visual elements. The HTML page 1 renders this as a near-identical image, but PDF page 2 (the second rendering of the instruction page with the form title below) shows the infographic followed by the D-400 Schedule PN-1 title. In the HTML, the infographic image appears to be present.

findings.description: Page 2 HTML combines the instruction page image with the beginning of the D-400 Schedule PN-1 form, whereas in the PDF these appear as separate visual elements on page 2
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows the instruction infographic at top followed by 'NCDOR Web 7-24', 'D-400 Schedule PN-1', '2024 Other Additions and Other Deductions' and beginning text. HTML page 2 shows the same content but the instruction image appears embedded differently.

findings.description: Part A table in the PDF has two distinct data columns (Column A and Column B) with entry fields showing '.00' placeholders. The HTML renders the same structure but Column A header text differs slightly.
findings.evidence: PDF Column A header: 'Amount from Form D-400 Schedule S'. HTML Column A header: 'COLUMN A Amount from Form D-400 Schedule S'. Both contain the same data but the HTML presents 'COLUMN A' as a separate label which is actually consistent with the PDF.

findings.description: The form header area with NCDOR logo, form title, DOR Use Only box, SSN fields, and Last Name field loses its multi-column layout in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a structured header with NCDOR logo on left, form title in center, DOR Use Only box on right, followed by Last Name field on left, Important notice in center, and SSN on right. HTML page 4 renders these as a simple table without the visual form layout.

findings.description: NCDOR logo is missing from the HTML rendering of the form page
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows the NCDOR logo at top left of the form. HTML page 3/4 does not display the logo.

findings.description: The form fields (entry boxes) for dollar amounts are not visually rendered as fillable fields in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows bordered entry fields with '.00' decimal indicators. HTML shows plain text '.00' values in table cells.

findings.description: Page 2 of the PDF form (Part B) header with Last Name, Tax Year 2024, and SSN fields loses its multi-column layout
findings.evidence: PDF page 5 shows 'Page 2', 'D-400 Sch. PN-1', 'Web 7-24' on left, 'Last Name' field, 'Tax Year 2024' centered, and 'SSN' on right. HTML page 5/6 renders these as simple stacked text elements.

findings.description: Part B table column headers differ slightly between PDF and HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'COLUMN A Amount from Form D-400 Schedule S' and 'COLUMN B Amount of Column A Attributable to N.C.' as structured column headers. HTML shows 'from Form D-400 Schedule S' and 'Column A Attributable to N.C.' - the header labels are slightly truncated.

findings.description: Line 11 'Reserved for Future Use' has a small entry box in the PDF form that is not present in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows a small checkbox or entry box next to 'Reserved for Future Use' on line 11. HTML shows plain text only.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is broken. 'NCDOR Web 7-24' is rendered as h2 before the h1 title, and the document has nested <main> elements. The h1 contains the schedule title which is acceptable, but having an h2 before h1 breaks the heading hierarchy.
findings.evidence:
HTML has

NCDOR Web 7-24

appearing before

D-400 Schedule PN-1
2024 Other Additions and Other Deductions

findings.description: The Part A table content appears to be duplicated. The PDF shows Part A once (on page 2), but the HTML appears to render it from both the compact page-2 layout and the full-size page-2 layout. However, examining more closely, the HTML only has one Part A table, so this is acceptable. The page header information from page 2 is consolidated, which is minor.
findings.evidence: After review, Part A table appears once in the HTML. No actual duplication found - withdrawing this finding.

findings.description: Line 27 for Taxed Pass-Through Entity Income has an empty row in the HTML table for the parent line item, with sub-rows 27a and 27b following. This is an acceptable structural representation but the parent row has empty cells for COLUMN A and COLUMN B rather than being a header-type row.
findings.evidence: HTML: '27. Taxed Pass-Through Entity Income' - PDF shows this as a label with 27a and 27b as sub-lines

findings.description: Nested <main> elements in the HTML - there is a <main> wrapping the entire content and another <main> inside it wrapping the tables. This is invalid HTML structure.

The column header comment '<!-- COLUMNS: Description | Line | COLUMN A Amount from Form D-400 Schedule S | COLUMN B Amount of Column A Attributable to N.C. -->' appears in both tables. These are HTML comments for developer reference and not visible content, so not fabrication per se.
findings.evidence: HTML comments in both table bodies - not visible to end users
